title: Replace static maps with interactive Leaflet maps and add hover, edit/delete, and zoom features

requirements.summary: Replace static map images with interactive Leaflet maps using OpenStreetMap tiles in both WorldMapPage and ATMapPage components
acceptanceCriteria: WorldMapPage renders an interactive Leaflet map with OpenStreetMap tiles
ATMapPage renders an interactive Leaflet map with OpenStreetMap tiles focused on the Appalachian Trail region
Existing entry pins are displayed on the Leaflet maps at their correct coordinates
Map tiles properly attribute OpenStreetMap contributors

file_operations.description: Add leaflet and react-leaflet dependencies (leaflet@^1.9.4, react-leaflet@^4.2.1) and @types/leaflet as a dev dependency for TypeScript support

Replace the static div-based map layout with a Leaflet MapContainer component using OpenStreetMap tiles. Configure the map to show a world view by default, add TileLayer with proper OSM attribution, and render Marker components for each entry's currentLocation. Remove the absolute-positioned MapPin components and replace them with Leaflet markers that maintain interactivity and selection state.

Replace the static background image (frontend/public/assets/generated/at-map-background.dim_1600x900.png) and MapPin layout with a Leaflet MapContainer focused on the Appalachian Trail region (centered approximately at [37.5, -81.5] with zoom level 6). Add TileLayer with OpenStreetMap tiles and proper attribution. Render Marker components for each entry's favoritePlace instead of absolute-positioned MapPin components. Remove the hiking trail logo image reference.

Create a custom Leaflet marker component that wraps the existing MapPin visual styling as a Leaflet DivIcon. Accept props for selected state, onClick handler, and optional tooltip content. This component bridges the existing MapPin appearance with Leaflet's marker system while supporting hover tooltips for place names.
Add Leaflet CSS import in the head section: <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://unpkg.com/leaflet@1.9.4/dist/leaflet.css" /> to ensure proper styling of Leaflet map controls and markers

requirements.summary: Display place name on hover over map pinpoints
acceptanceCriteria: Hovering over a map pin displays the decoded place name from the entry
Tooltip appears immediately on hover and disappears on mouse leave
Place name is extracted using the existing decodePlaceNameFromComment utility

file_operations.description: For each entry marker, extract the place name using decodePlaceNameFromComment(entry.comment) and pass it to the LeafletMapPin component or Leaflet Tooltip component. Configure the tooltip to appear on hover with bindTooltip or Tooltip component wrapping each Marker.

For each entry marker, extract the place name using decodePlaceNameFromComment(entry.comment) and pass it to the LeafletMapPin component or Leaflet Tooltip component. Configure the tooltip to appear on hover with bindTooltip or Tooltip component wrapping each Marker.

Add support for rendering a Leaflet Tooltip when tooltip content is provided via props. Ensure the tooltip displays immediately on hover and disappears on mouse leave by configuring appropriate Leaflet tooltip options.

requirements.summary: Add edit and delete abilities to guestbook entries
acceptanceCriteria: Users can delete their own entries from the entry detail page
EditEntryDialog functionality is accessible from appropriate locations in the UI
Delete operations are protected so users can only delete their own entries
After deletion, user is redirected to the feed and the entry is removed from all views

file_operations.description: Add a delete button next to the existing edit button that is visible only when the current user is the entry creator (using identity.getPrincipal().toString() === entry.creator.toString()). Wire the delete button to call the deleteEntry mutation from useQueries with the entry's timestamp. On successful deletion, navigate the user back to the guestbook feed page. Show a confirmation dialog before deleting to prevent accidental deletions.

Add a useDeleteEntry mutation hook that calls actor.deleteEntry(timestamp). Configure the mutation to invalidate the 'entries' and 'entriesWithLocation' and 'entriesWithFavoritePlace' query keys on success to ensure all entry lists refresh after deletion. Return the mutation function and status for use in components.

Ensure the EditEntryDialog is already accessible from the entry detail page and functions correctly with the existing updateEntry mutation. Verify that the dialog properly handles the encoded place-name system and location updates. No changes needed if already functional.

requirements.summary: Remove the generic hiking trail logo from the AT pin board
acceptanceCriteria: ATMapPage no longer displays a static hiking trail logo image
The map area is clean and focuses on the interactive Leaflet map

file_operations.description: Remove any img or background-image references to hiking trail logos (likely referencing assets in frontend/public/assets/generated/ with names like at-outline-map or similar). Ensure the component only renders the interactive Leaflet map without decorative static images overlaying or surrounding it.

requirements.summary: Use actual map backgrounds for both World and AT boards that correspond to the pinned points
acceptanceCriteria: WorldMapPage displays a real OpenStreetMap background centered on existing pins
ATMapPage displays a real OpenStreetMap background focused on the Appalachian Trail region
Maps automatically adjust their view to show all pinned locations when entries exist
Empty maps show a sensible default view (world view for World map, AT region for AT map)

file_operations.description: Implement automatic map bounds calculation using Leaflet's fitBounds when entries with currentLocation exist. Calculate the bounding box from all entry coordinates and call map.fitBounds to center and zoom the map to show all pins. If no entries exist, default to a world view (center [0, 0] with zoom level 2). The OpenStreetMap TileLayer already provides the real map background.

Implement automatic map bounds calculation using Leaflet's fitBounds when entries with favoritePlace exist. Calculate the bounding box from all entry coordinates and call map.fitBounds to center and zoom the map to show all pins. If no entries exist, default to the Appalachian Trail region view (center approximately [37.5, -81.5] with zoom level 6). The OpenStreetMap TileLayer already provides the real map background instead of the static image frontend/public/assets/generated/at-map-background.dim_1600x900.png.

requirements.summary: Add zoom in/out functionality to both interactive maps
acceptanceCriteria: Both WorldMapPage and ATMapPage support mouse wheel zoom
Maps include visible zoom control buttons (+ and -)
Zoom functionality works smoothly and maintains pin positions
Zoom levels are appropriate for viewing individual pins and overall geography

file_operations.description: Ensure the MapContainer has scrollWheelZoom enabled and includes a ZoomControl component from react-leaflet. Configure appropriate min and max zoom levels (e.g., minZoom: 2, maxZoom: 18) to allow viewing from world geography down to individual pin locations. The zoom control buttons (+ and -) are provided by default by Leaflet's ZoomControl.

Ensure the MapContainer has scrollWheelZoom enabled and includes a ZoomControl component from react-leaflet. Configure appropriate min and max zoom levels (e.g., minZoom: 5, maxZoom: 18) to allow viewing the full AT region down to individual favorite place pins. The zoom control buttons (+ and -) are provided by default by Leaflet's ZoomControl.
